old_claim.note: Labor Unions & Collective Bargaining provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: No direct labor-union dependency remains because the node is scoped to early statutory factory safety regulation and inspection, not collective bargaining institutions.

source_shape.source_locator: UK Parliament's Early factory legislation page describes factory legislation and inspection as parliamentary responses to factory conditions.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source supports statutory factory regulation and inspection, not labor unions as a direct prerequisite.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Labor unions are a later or parallel worker-organization pathway; retaining the edge would make collective bargaining a prerequisite for early legislation.

ontology_before: Industrial Safety Regulation depended on Labor Unions & Collective Bargaining as an unsourced enabling edge.
ontology_after: Industrial Safety Regulation depends on the factory system and public-health statistics, keeping statutory safety regulation separate from labor organization.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: labor_unions_collective_bargaining is no longer a direct prerequisite for industrial_safety_regulation.
would_reject_if: The node were narrowed to collectively bargained safety agreements rather than statutory regulation.
A stronger source showed early factory safety legislation directly required labor unions.
A separate union_safety_bargaining node were added and scoped to collective bargaining.
short_reason: Labor unions are worker-organization context, not statutory safety infrastructure.
